title: bursera queretarensis
intro: Bursera queretarensis, also known as the Mexican copal, is a small tree or shrub native to Mexico and Central America, belonging to the family Burseraceae. The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, particularly in traditional Mexican medicine, due to its antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial properties. The health benefits of Bursera queretarensis include its ability to soothe respiratory issues, reduce fever, and alleviate digestive problems, as well as its potential to lower blood pressure and improve cardiovascular health. Bursera queretarensis can be prepared as a tea, infusion, or resin, which can be used to treat various ailments, and is also used in aromatherapy due to its pleasant, woody fragrance.
